headline: Dorsey's Job Cuts at Block Arouse Suspicions of AI-Washing
summary: When Block laid off nearly half its staff last week, co-founder Jack Dorsey offered a seemingly simple explanation: artificial intelligence was allowing the company to do more with fewer employees. The announcement, though, landed at the center of a complex debate over AI and the future of work: on one side, genuine fear that the technology will displace jobs at an unprecedented pace; on the other, deep cynicism that companies are exploiting that fear to dress up old-fashioned cost-cutting as technological futurism. Marcelo P. Lima, Founder and Managing Partner at Heller House, joins Bloomberg Businessweek Daily to discuss. Lima has been a vocal critic of Dorsey's management style at Block, and posted soon after the layoff announcement that the reduction in force "is the new Citrini fake narrative." Lima speaks with Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec.

headline: Peter Thiel Once Explained Why His Intuition On AI Defies Mainstream Thinking: 'Worse For The Math People Than The Word People'
summary: Peter Thiel argues that advances in artificial intelligence could shift societal power structures by reducing the dominance of math-based credentials, potentially favoring "word skills" instead, while broader concerns about automation and layoffs signal growing workforce disruption.

headline: Fed races to adapt to AI promises and pitfalls for jobs, inflation
summary: U.S. Federal Reserve officials who have largely accepted that artificial intelligence will lead to dramatic shifts in the economy are now struggling to understand the pace and extent of what's to come, with a divide emerging over its potential to impact the labor market and prices.  The AI transition, though, has raised a different response, with officials ‌saying higher unemployment rates may be par for the course ahead, with displaced workers taking longer to find new jobs and the higher capital returns and wages for those still working keeping upward pressure on inflation.  That group includes Fed chair nominee Kevin Warsh, who feels interest rates should fall in part to account for AI-driven productivity gains holding down inflation.

raw.headline: Fed races to adapt to AI promises and pitfalls for jobs, inflation

headline: AI "Wrecking Ball" Keeps Swinging Through Markets
summary: AI was a wrecking ball last week, separating winners and losers. One example: Block Inc. (XYZ). Less than a week after a Citrini report came out warning about a dystopian AI future characterized by huge job losses, Block announced it was cutting 40% of its workforce in a bet that AI can replace those workers, notes Amber Kanwar, host of the In the Money with Amber Kanwar podcast.

headline: Block (SQ) Is Up 19.7% After Massive AI-Led Restructuring And 40% Workforce Cut – What's Changed
summary: In late February 2026, Block, Inc. reported full-year 2025 results showing revenue of US$24.19 billion and net income of US$1.31 billion, alongside plans to cut more than 40% of its workforce as part of a large restructuring tied to artificial intelligence tools. The company expects US$450 million to US$500 million in restructuring charges as it moves toward a smaller, AI-centric operating model that management says is driven by productivity gains rather than financial distress.
